Internal Penetration Testing

We test what happens after an attacker gets inside your network. This is about understanding your exposure from within.

What It Covers

  • Internal network architecture review
  • Workstation and server assessment
  • Privilege escalation paths
  • Lateral movement possibilities
  • Active Directory security
  • Credential harvesting risks

Our Approach

We simulate an attacker who has gained initial access—either through a phished credential, a compromised endpoint, or physical access. From there, we see how far we can go and what we can access.

Deliverables

  • Findings report with proof of access
  • Network architecture recommendations
  • Remediation roadmap
  • Executive summary
  • Debrief call

Typical Timeline

Internal tests typically take 2-3 weeks depending on network size and complexity.

What You'll Need

  • Scope definition (network ranges)
  • Test account (domain user)
  • Network documentation
